customCode.withoutCompile: Customer-contract pricing is the zero-code headline: the buyer-scoped EcomPrices row (PACK-RPP-0004, PriceUserCustomerNumber 98745621) is resolved and charged end-to-end by Dynamicweb's stock DefaultPriceProvider with NO custom code — PACK-RPP-PROD2 prices 1399 for customer 98745621 (base 1599). The quantity-tier EcomPrices rows (PACK-RPP-0001..0003 on PACK-RPP-PROD1) ship as data and render on tier-aware surfaces, but the stock cart resolver ignores tier quantities at cart time, so uncompiled the cart charges the base price for tiers.
customCode.withCompile: Compiling ReorderingPricingQtyBreakProvider (PriceProvider, discovered by assembly-scan; non-exclusive, HandlePricesExclusively=false) ADDS cart-time enforcement of the quantity tiers: PACK-RPP-PROD1 prices 4500/4200/3900 at qty 5/10/25. The provider returns a price ONLY for real tiers (Quantity > 1); base and contract rows fall through to the default provider, so contract pricing is identical whether or not it is compiled.
customCode.compileStep: Opt-in per the dynamicweb-skills feature-layer compile recipe (D3): add src/*.cs to the Swift solution's custom-code project and build; the provider self-registers via assembly scan (no config row required).

configRows.description: qty-break tier row (PACK-RPP-PROD1, qty 5, unit 4500 EUR — base 4995)
qty-break tier row (PACK-RPP-PROD1, qty 10, unit 4200 EUR)
qty-break tier row (PACK-RPP-PROD1, qty 25, unit 3900 EUR)
contract row for buyer 98745621 (PACK-RPP-PROD2, qty 1, unit 1399 EUR — base 1599; PriceUserCustomerNumber scoping only)
